label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12

extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ attacked from 32-rider breakaway on final climb, won solo while EVENEPOEL answered all GC attacks to retain red jersey despite earlier crash.
tactical_patterns: BATTISTELLA solo bridge attempt 65km out from oversized break failed; group too large to cooperate on chasing lone ranger.
FABBRO paced KELDERMAN up final climb despite being breakaway rider himself, revealing team willingness to sacrifice stage contention for GC.
Quick-Step isolated EVENEPOEL early on final climb yet he responded personally to attacks rather than teammates controlling pace.
EVENEPOEL counterattacked at summit after absorbing pressure, signaling confidence in finishing power over sustained climbing defense.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Stage win as Ineos team principal suggests freedom from GC support role unlocks offensive capabilities.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Maintained GC gaps despite crash and isolated position on climb, showing resilience under repeated attacks.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Moved into top-six GC with dedicated team pacing from FABBRO, indicating form building mid-race.
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Finished 7th from break after winning two prior stages, suggesting accumulated fatigue limits consecutive breakthrough efforts.
rider_signals.POLANC Jan: positive: Struggled initially but recovered to finish 4th, demonstrating veteran ability to manage efforts on long climbs.
rider_signals.BATTISTELLA Samuele: neutral: Solo attack 65km out showed ambition but lack of cooperation from 32-rider break made success impossible.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: neutral: Team attacked repeatedly but failed to distance EVENEPOEL, suggesting current form insufficient to break race leader.
